title: Miconia sparsisetulosa

This family consists of around 170 genera and more than 5,000 species. Within the family Melastomataceae, Miconia sparsisetulosa is classified as a member of the genus Miconia, which is one of the largest genera in the family with over 1,500 species. The genus Miconia is a group of shrubs and small trees, often with brightly colored flowers. Miconia sparsisetulosa is a specific species within this genus and family, but its exact classification is more specific and may require further research.
